evals.prompt: Add Label Capture to my React Native app. Here's my screen file: empty-app.tsx. I need to scan a Code 128 barcode and an expiry date on a perishable product label.
evals.expected_output: The skill walks the user through the Interactive Label Definition (Question A/B/C), then writes complete Label Capture integration code into empty-app.tsx as a function component with hooks. A Validation Flow overlay is wired up. The setup checklist is shown.

assertions.text: Interactive Label Definition asks the user which fields are on the label (Question A)
Setup checklist mentions scandit-react-native-datacapture-core, scandit-react-native-datacapture-barcode, and scandit-react-native-datacapture-label
Setup checklist mentions npx pod-install for iOS
Setup checklist mentions NSCameraUsageDescription in ios Info.plist
DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present
DataCaptureContext.sharedInstance is used to obtain the context
A license key placeholder string is present (semantic)
CustomBarcode.initWithNameAndSymbologies( is present
Symbology.Code128 is enabled on the CustomBarcode
new ExpiryDateText( is present
new LabelDefinition( is present
The label definition's fields array is assigned (semantic)
LabelCaptureSettings.settingsFromLabelDefinitions( is called
new LabelCapture(settings) is present (no LabelCaptureSettingsBuilder)
LabelCaptureSettingsBuilder is NOT present
LabelDefinitionBuilder is NOT present
Factory function shorthand like label(...) or customBarcode(...) is NOT used (web-only)
<DataCaptureView is rendered as JSX with context
LabelCaptureBasicOverlay or LabelCaptureValidationFlowOverlay is added via view.addOverlay(...)
didCaptureLabelWithFields callback is registered (via listener) — NOT onValidationFlowLabelCaptured
onValidationFlowLabelCaptured is NOT present
onManualInput is NOT present
The component is written as a function component (no class syntax)
useRef is used to hold the LabelCapture and view references
AppState.addEventListener handles background/foreground transitions
Imports come from scandit-react-native-datacapture-core, scandit-react-native-datacapture-barcode, and scandit-react-native-datacapture-label
LabelCapture.createRecommendedCameraSettings() is used to obtain the recommended camera settings
A Camera is created and set as the frame source via dataCaptureContext.setFrameSource( (e.g. Camera.withSettings(...) or Camera.default)
camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.On) is called to turn the camera on

evals.prompt: Add Label Capture with the Validation Flow overlay so the user can confirm OCR results and manually correct fields if needed. Use the redesigned flow. My package.json says I'm on 8.4.0.
evals.expected_output: The skill recognises the v8.2+ redesigned Validation Flow, uses LabelCaptureValidationFlowOverlay, and registers a listener with both didCaptureLabelWithFields and didSubmitManualInputForField. Optionally registers didUpdateValidationFlowResult since the user is on 8.4+.

assertions.text: LabelCaptureValidationFlowOverlay is constructed and added via view.addOverlay(...)
didCaptureLabelWithFields callback is registered on the listener
didSubmitManualInputForField callback is registered on the listener (v8.2+)
didUpdateValidationFlowResult is either registered or explicitly noted as optional and 8.4+
onValidationFlowLabelCaptured is NOT present
onManualInput is NOT present
DataCaptureContext.initialize( is used
License key placeholder is present

evals.prompt: Add Label Capture with just the basic overlay (no validation flow). My package.json says I'm on 8.2.0. I want to scan a CustomBarcode plus a TotalPriceText field.
evals.expected_output: The skill writes a Label Capture integration that uses LabelCaptureBasicOverlay (no Validation Flow). The mode listener handles didUpdateSession and reads captured labels from the session.

assertions.text: LabelCaptureBasicOverlay is constructed and added via view.addOverlay(...)
LabelCaptureValidationFlowOverlay is NOT used
labelCapture.addListener( is called with a listener that includes didUpdateSession
session.capturedLabels is read inside didUpdateSession to extract labels
CustomBarcode.initWithNameAndSymbologies( is present
new TotalPriceText( is present
new LabelDefinition( is present and labelDefinition.fields = [...] is set
LabelCaptureSettings.settingsFromLabelDefinitions( is called
DataCaptureContext.initialize( is used

evals.prompt: I'm building a Label Capture label in my React Native app. The label has a batch number printed as text in the form FZ12345 (5 to 10 digits after the FZ prefix), with the word 'Batch' printed next to it. There's no pre-made field for this. How do I capture it?
evals.expected_output: The skill uses CustomText (no pre-built equivalent exists for a batch number) and configures it with valueRegexes for the value pattern and anchorRegexes for the 'Batch' keyword. It assembles the field into a LabelDefinition and builds LabelCaptureSettings.

assertions.text: new CustomText( is used for the batch number field (no pre-built field fits)
A value regex with the FZ-prefixed digit pattern is set via field.valueRegexes or field.valueRegex (semantic)
field.anchorRegexes = [...] is set with the 'Batch' keyword to disambiguate the field
new LabelDefinition( is present and the CustomText field is added to its fields
LabelCaptureSettings.settingsFromLabelDefinitions( is called
Imports come from scandit-react-native-datacapture-label

evals.prompt: In my React Native Label Capture app, the barcode is always present but the total price is sometimes missing from the label. How do I mark the total price as optional, and how do I safely read it when it might not be there?
evals.expected_output: The skill sets field.optional = true on the TotalPriceText field (and notes required is the default — omitting it makes a field required). When reading the captured field it null-checks before using the value, because an optional field may be absent.

assertions.text: field.optional = true is set on the TotalPriceText (optional) field
The response notes that required is the default and omitting field.optional (or setting it false) makes the field required
The barcode field is left required (field.optional = false or simply not marked optional)
When reading the captured value, the code null-checks the field/value (semantic)

evals.prompt: Add Label Capture for a hard disk drive label in my React Native app. The label has a serial number and a part number, both printed as Code 128 barcodes. Write the field definitions.
evals.expected_output: The skill uses the pre-built semantic barcode fields SerialNumberBarcode and PartNumberBarcode (rather than two generic CustomBarcode fields), enabling Code 128, and assembles them into a LabelDefinition + LabelCaptureSettings.

assertions.text: SerialNumberBarcode is used for the serial number field (not a generic CustomBarcode)
PartNumberBarcode is used for the part number field (not a generic CustomBarcode)
Symbology.Code128 is enabled on the semantic barcode fields
new LabelDefinition( is present and both semantic fields are added to its fields
LabelCaptureSettings.settingsFromLabelDefinitions( is called

evals.prompt: I just want to scan a retail price tag (price, unit price, weight) in my React Native app. Is there a ready-made label definition for that so I don't have to define every field myself?
evals.expected_output: The skill recommends the pre-built price-capture label definition via LabelDefinition.createPriceCaptureDefinition(name) and builds LabelCaptureSettings from it directly, without defining individual fields. It notes the definition is sealed (do not addField to it).

assertions.text: LabelDefinition.createPriceCaptureDefinition( is used to create the pre-built price label definition
LabelCaptureSettings.settingsFromLabelDefinitions( is called with the pre-built definition
The response does NOT define individual price/unit-price/weight fields by hand — it uses the sealed pre-built definition
The response notes the pre-built definition should not be mutated with .addField(...) (it is sealed native-side)

evals.prompt: I'm using LabelCaptureBasicOverlay in my React Native Label Capture app. I want to highlight the Barcode field in cyan and the Expiry Date field in orange, and leave everything else transparent. How do I customize the field highlight colors?
evals.expected_output: The skill implements a LabelCaptureBasicOverlayListener and returns a per-field Brush from brushForFieldOfLabel based on field.name, optionally returning a brush from brushForLabel. It assigns the listener to the overlay. Brush/Color come from scandit-react-native-datacapture-core.

assertions.text: A LabelCaptureBasicOverlayListener object is created and assigned to overlay.listener
brushForFieldOfLabel is implemented and returns a per-field Brush (e.g. switching on field.name for Barcode vs Expiry Date)
brushForLabel is implemented on the listener
new Brush( is constructed with Color (e.g. Color.fromRGBA( or Color.fromHex() ) for the field highlights
Brush and Color are imported from scandit-react-native-datacapture-core

evals.prompt: In my React Native Label Capture app I want to draw a custom 'expires soon' warning view anchored below the expiry date field, as a live AR overlay on the camera feed. How do I do that?
evals.expected_output: The skill uses LabelCaptureAdvancedOverlay with a listener implementing viewForCapturedLabelField (returning a React Native view or null), plus anchor/offset callbacks (anchorForCapturedLabelField, offsetForCapturedLabelField). It assigns the listener to the overlay.

assertions.text: LabelCaptureAdvancedOverlay is used for the live AR / custom-view overlay
A listener implementing viewForCapturedLabelField is provided to return the custom view (or null for fields that should not be decorated)
anchorForCapturedLabelField returns an Anchor (e.g. Anchor.BottomCenter) to position the view relative to the field
offsetForCapturedLabelField returns a PointWithUnit offset for the view
The listener is assigned to the overlay (overlay.listener = ...)

evals.prompt: My customer's React Native Label Capture app struggles with worn, low-contrast shipping labels and the on-device OCR keeps missing fields. Can the cloud help, and how do I turn it on?
evals.expected_output: The skill explains the Adaptive Recognition Engine (cloud fallback) and enables it by setting label.adaptiveRecognitionMode = AdaptiveRecognitionMode.Auto on the LabelDefinition. It flags that ARE is a beta API and that production access requires contacting support@scandit.com.

assertions.text: The response identifies the Adaptive Recognition Engine (ARE) / cloud fallback as the mechanism
label.adaptiveRecognitionMode = AdaptiveRecognitionMode.Auto is set on the LabelDefinition to enable it
AdaptiveRecognitionMode is imported from scandit-react-native-datacapture-label
The response flags that the Adaptive Recognition Engine is a BETA API that may change in future SDK versions
The response states production use requires contacting support@scandit.com to enable ARE on the subscription

evals.prompt: I want to scan full grocery receipts in my React Native app and get structured data (store name, line items, total). Is that possible with Label Capture, and how?
evals.expected_output: The skill explains receipt scanning is available via the Adaptive Recognition Engine using LabelCaptureAdaptiveRecognitionOverlay + LabelCaptureAdaptiveRecognitionListener (onRecognized returns a ReceiptScanningResult). It flags that this is a beta feature requiring support@scandit.com to enable, and that it uses a different overlay than standard label capture.

assertions.text: LabelCaptureAdaptiveRecognitionOverlay is used as the receipt-scanning overlay (a different overlay than the standard Basic/Validation Flow overlays)
A LabelCaptureAdaptiveRecognitionListener is used with an onRecognized callback that receives a ReceiptScanningResult
The response references ReceiptScanningResult fields (e.g. storeName, lineItems, paymentTotal)
The response flags that Receipt Scanning / the Adaptive Recognition Engine is a BETA feature that may change in future SDK versions
The response states enabling it requires contacting support@scandit.com

evals.prompt: On React Native my Smart Label Capture preview is black on Android, and on iOS where the preview works the expiry-date and total-price fields never get populated although the barcode scans fine. How do I fix it?
evals.expected_output: The skill diagnoses the black preview as the runtime CAMERA permission not being granted (via PermissionsAndroid) / NSCameraUsageDescription missing or the camera never being switched on, and the empty text fields as the ScanditLabelCaptureText / ScanditPriceLabel modules not being bundled (scandit-react-native-datacapture-label missing or pods not re-installed). It does not invent new APIs.

assertions.text: The black/blank preview is attributed to the camera permission not being granted (Android runtime CAMERA via PermissionsAndroid.request(PermissionsAndroid.PERMISSIONS.CAMERA) / iOS NSCameraUsageDescription in Info.plist) and/or camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.On) never being called

The empty expiry-date and total-price text fields are attributed to the missing on-device text models — the ScanditLabelCaptureText module (arbitrary text) and ScanditPriceLabel module (price fields), which ship in scandit-react-native-datacapture-label; the fix is to ensure that package is installed and the iOS pods are re-installed (cd ios && pod install)
The response notes the barcode field works without the text models because barcode-only fields do not require them
The response does NOT invent new diagnostic APIs or configuration flags that are not in the Scandit references
